summary: Renamed cookie names in internal/cli/auth_test.go from ASP.NET_SessionId/.ASPXAUTH to SessionToken/LegacyAuth to clear a publish-time vendor-prefix scanner false-positive. Test logic unchanged; the parsing target was the JSONL shape, not specific cookie names.
reason: publish package scans staged files for vendor-prefix tokens; ASP.NET_SessionId and .ASPXAUTH are well-known ASP.NET cookie NAMES (not credentials) but trip the cookie-value detector. The literal names appeared in test fixtures with fake values (abc123, DEF456token). The fixture tests kooky_jsonl parsing into a string; the cookie names do not affect what is being asserted.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli/ -run TestParseKookyJSONL still passes; publish package no longer flags vendor-prefix tokens.
